mnemonics: A treasure map where 'X' doesn't mark the spot, but points to the next map.
Think of a Russian nesting doll that holds the address of the next doll, not the doll itself.
keyPoints: A structure that contains a pointer to a structure of its own type.
It is the fundamental building block for linked data structures like linked lists and trees.
The structure is defined recursively, with the pointer member allowing the structure to refer to another instance of itself.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the 'next' pointer to NULL, causing undefined behavior when traversing the list.
Incorrectly managing memory (malloc/free) for each node, leading to memory leaks or corruption.
Assuming the size of the structure includes the size of the pointed-to structure, which it does not; the pointer is just an address.
